mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 0.25 percent suppresses risk-free yields and lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of all observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.0 percent signals persistent labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply momentum that boosts earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both rising month-over-month
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the joint signal does not generate positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if a linear model with an interaction term shows no significant incremental fit (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects alone.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries with unemployment above 7 percent, month-over-month payroll employment growth signals healing labor markets and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with unemployment above 7 percent
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months during high-unemployment recovery regimes, or if the signal's information coefficient is insignificant (t-stat < 1.5) in those regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 650 thousand units indicate resurgent residential investment and construction activity that spills over into broader economic demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal is rejected if S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances when HOUST exceeds 650, or if the win rate is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A higher level of industrial production reflects greater manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which is positively associated with aggregate corporate earnings and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the rank correlation between INDPRO level and 60-day forward returns is non-positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25 percent while consumer prices rise month-over-month, negative real policy rates and stagflation fears dominate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the joint condition does not produce negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term does not significantly improve prediction (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that supports top-line corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth.
